title: Mondo-Mod
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Mondo-Mod.jpg]] |name=Mondo-Mod |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]{{CWACite|8|Versus (comic story)}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |skin=Brown |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Arena of Doom]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|noquote=1|quoteend=0|«Are you ready, Jedi?''»
"''Yes.''"
«''Then let the battle begin!»|Mondo-Mod and Luminara Unduli|}} A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], '''Mondo-Mod''' was the owner of the [[Arena of Doom]], a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] fighting ring, in the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Mondo-Mod came into possession of the coordinates for a secret [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederate]] [[Diorda weapons factory|weapons factory]] on [[Diorda]], and was subsequently approached by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]] for the coordinates. Mondo-Mod agreed to give Unduli the factory's location if she defeated three of his best gladiators in the Arena of Doom without using her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] or [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]], a task that the Hutt thought to be impossible. Despite Mondo-Mod's belief to the contrary, Unduli managed to best her three opponents—a [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]], a [[durkii]], and the [[Gladiator droid/Legends|gladiator droid]] [[Evil Supreme]]—in three separate matches, forcing Mondo-Mod to reluctantly supply the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] with the information she requested. Mondo-Mod was additionally left with his [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] accounts depleted, as Unduli's former [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], had won a hefty amount of credits from him by [[Gambling/Legends|betting]] on the successful Unduli in the final match. ==Biography== {{Quote|You've done well. You've made it quite a night at my arena… but you're tired and badly hurt. You'll lose now. Don't feel bad, many great fighters have died here. Now you will join them. Goodbye, Jedi.|translated=1|Mondo-Mod, to Luminara Unduli|}} Mondo-Mod was a wealthy{{CSWECite|II|333|Mondo-Mod}} [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] business executive who lived during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], Mondo-Mod owned and operated the [[Arena of Doom]], a [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] fighting ring that became known for its extravagant matches. Dozens of spectators and fans came to watch Mondo-Mod's gladiatorial competitions, and the Hutt grew [[Gender/Legends|his]] fortune with the [[Gambling/Legends|bets]] that onlookers placed on the matches. [[File:Arena_of_Doom.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Mondo-Mod announces for the fight to begin.]] During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Mondo-Mod acquired the location of a secret Confederate [[Diorda weapons factory|weapons factory]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Diorda]]. He was soon approached by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]], who wished to purchase the location of the Confederate arms factory from him. Mondo-Mod agreed to supply the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] with the coordinates, but not until she completed a task for him: Unduli had to defeat three of the Hutt's greatest gladiators in the Arena of Doom, without benefit of her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] or utilization of [[Force power/Legends|powers]] over [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Mondo-Mod was certain that the Jedi Master would lose one of the matches and be [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Unduli accepted Mondo-Mod's challenge, and she was soon prepared to fight in the Arena of Doom against her first opponent, a [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] from the planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. With a crowd of spectators watching and betting on the match, Mondo-Mod—overlooking the fighting pit from a pedestal above the arena—ordered that the fight begin. When the wampa charged at her, Unduli grabbed the creature's horns and threw the beast into the arena's wall, immobilizing it. Despite Mondo-Mod's interjection that she had cheated by using her Force abilities against the wampa, Unduli assured him that she had used simple physics to turn the wampa's momentum and size against itself. Mondo-Mod grudgingly allowed her to continue with the challenge. The next opponent was a large [[durkii]], which struck Unduli with its massive [[Limb/Legends|tail]] and seriously injured her. When Unduli ran between the durkii's legs, the creature attempted to follow her as she did so, subsequently rolling over and crashing heavily onto the ground. With the durkii bested, Unduli was allowed to move on to her third match, to Mondo-Mod's irritation. The Hutt did not believe, though, that Unduli could defeat her final opponent, as she was too tired and badly injured. [[File:Evil_Supreme.jpg|thumb|right|225px|Unduli takes on Evil Supreme.]] Unduli's last competitor was a large, multi-limbed [[Gladiator droid/Legends|gladiator droid]] named [[Evil Supreme]] that towered over her. However, Unduli kicked a small pebble into the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] faceplate, destroying its primary circuits and disabling the droid. Mondo-Mod was shocked that Unduli had completed his challenge, and he reluctantly provided her with the coordinates to the Confederate weapons factory. The Hutt had Unduli escorted out of the Arena of Doom and ordered that she never return. Unbeknownst to Mondo-Mod, Unduli's former [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], was at the Arena of Doom during the fights, disguised as an anonymous patron. For Unduli's match against Evil Supreme, Offee placed a bet of ten million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on her former Master. After Unduli defeated Evil Supreme, [[Sniddly]]—one of Mondo-Mod's underlings—was forced to pay off Offee's wager, depleting Mondo-Mod's entire fortune. Sniddly later reported the loss to Mondo-Mod, and the Hutt was forced to close the Arena of Doom until he could recover his losses. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I hate Jedi.|translated=1|Mondo-Mod, after losing his entire fortune|}} A male Hutt, Mondo-Mod enjoyed watching Luminara Unduli fight in the Arena of Doom and was sure prior to Unduli's victory over her three opponents that the Jedi Master would die during his challenge. However, the Hutt believed that there was no dishonor in dying in the Arena of Doom, as many great warriors had fallen in the fighting pit. After he learned that all of his wealth had been lost due to Unduli's victory, Mondo-Mod expressed his hatred for Jedi. He had [[Color/Legends|green]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], brown skin, and lacked [[Hair/Legends|hair]]. Although he understood [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], he mainly spoke a different [[Language/Legends|language]]. ==Behind the scenes== Mondo-Mod first appeared in [[Versus (comic story)|''Versus'']], an eighteen-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] in ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures Volume 8]]'', which was released on [[June 20]], [[2007]]. Mondo-Mod later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' in [[December]] [[2008]]. ==Appearances== *{{CWACite|8|Versus (comic story)}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Mondo-Mod |nl=Mondo-Mod }} [[Category:Business owners and executives]] [[Category:Hutts]] [[Category:Males]]
